title: Tarenna grevei
taxonomy: Tarenna grevei is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It belongs to the genus Tarenna, which consists of tropical plants found in Africa and Asia. The plant's classification places it in the order Gentianales, which is a group of flowering plants known for their distinct characteristics. At the kingdom level, Tarenna grevei is classified as a plant, specifically a flowering plant. Its classification is based on various morphological and genetic features that distinguish it from other plant species.

intro: Tarenna grevei, also known as Tarenna or 'Ceylon Tea Tree', is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, as well as its potential to aid in the treatment of fever and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Tarenna grevei include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other secondary metabolites, which are thought to contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Tarenna grevei is often made into teas, infusions, or decoctions, as well as being used in the form of tinctures or dried extracts.
